channel.title: AI Unfiltered
channel.description: Chinese AI • Open Source • Security • Incidents. Signal, not noise.

item.title: From Explicit Elements to Implicit Intent: A Predefined Library for Auditable Behavioral Inference
item.description: arXiv:2606.11207v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present SemantiClean, a modular framework for extracting structured semantic signals from e-commerce session data and driving pluggable inference targets including purchase intent, customer segmentation, and product affinity through a shared...

item.title: Position: Hippocampal Explicit Memory Is the Cornerstone for AGI
item.description: arXiv:2606.11245v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have demonstrated remarkable capabilities across various tasks, raising expectations for Artificial General Intelligence (AGI). This position paper argues that integrating explicit memory is the cornerstone for advancing...

item.title: Can AI Agents Synthesize Scientific Conclusions?
item.description: arXiv:2606.11337v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Scientific AI agents increasingly retrieve evidence, reason across sources, and synthesize conclusions used in consequential decisions. Yet, their ability to do so in high-stakes domains such as health remains unclear. We introduce SciConBench, a...

item.title: Knowing When to Ask: Self-Gated Clarification for Hierarchical Language Agents
item.description: arXiv:2606.11349v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: In hierarchical reasoning, failures often originate at intermediate decision points where the agent commits to a wrong branch without recognizing that it lacks critical information. Rather than treating clarification as an external uncertainty...

item.title: Automated Mediator for Human Negotiation: Pre-Mediation via a Structured LLM Pipeline
item.description: arXiv:2606.11379v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Pre-mediation, the preparatory phase preceding direct human negotiation, plays a critical role in achieving mutually beneficial agreements, yet is often omitted due to cost, time, and limited access to trained mediators. We introduce an automated...

item.title: Restless bandits with imperfect binary feedback: PCL-indexability analysis and computation
item.description: arXiv:2606.11192v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We study restless bandits with binary latent states and imperfect binary feedback, motivated by opportunistic spectrum access with sensing errors. For the associated belief-state model, we develop a partial conservation laws (PCL)-based analytical and...

item.title: To Intervene or Not: Guiding Inference-time Alignment with Probabilistic Model Blending
item.description: arXiv:2606.11201v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The wide deployment of LLMs has made model alignment necessary to make newly trained models safely and effectively respond to user instructions. Among different methods, inference-time alignment is often cheaper as it intervenes (i.e., offers...

item.title: Dual-Stance Evaluation of Sycophancy: The Structure of Agreement and the Limits of Intervention
item.description: arXiv:2606.11205v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Activation steering can shift LLM behaviour, but standard evaluations do not typically test whether a sycophancy-reduction direction also suppresses agreement with factually correct statements. We introduce dual-stance evaluation, which tests both...

item.title: Few-Shot Resampling for Scalable Statistically-Sound Data Mining
item.description: arXiv:2606.11235v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: A key step in knowledge discovery is the evaluation of data mining results. In several applications, including pattern mining, graph analysis, and others, this step includes the evaluation of the statistical significance of the results, to avoid...

item.title: ProHiFlo: Hierarchical Flow Matching with Functional Guidance for De Novo Protein Generation
item.description: arXiv:2606.11243v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: De novo protein generation has transformative potential in therapeutic design, enzyme engineering, and synthetic biology. While diffusion-based and flow matching approaches have achieved progress, they typically operate at single resolution and lack...

item.title: PoQ-Judge: A Multi-Architecture Evaluation Framework for Cost-Aware Proof-of-Quality in Decentralized LLM Inference
item.description: arXiv:2606.11196v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Decentralized LLM inference networks need lightweight, reference-free quality evaluation for Proof of Quality (PoQ). We present PoQ-Judge, a framework that trains dedicated judge models to score query-output pairs without ground-truth references. We...

item.title: The Structural Attention Tax: How Retrieval Format Hijacks In-Context Learning Independent of Content
item.description: arXiv:2606.11198v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) systems inject external knowledge to improve LLM outputs, yet the format of injected content -- distinct from its semantic relevance -- can independently distort the model's attention distribution. We identify and...

item.title: NightFeats @ MMU-RAGent NeurIPS 2025: A Context-Optimized Multi-Agent RAG System for the Text-to-Text Track
item.description: arXiv:2606.11199v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present NightFeats, a structured multi-agent retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) system submitted to the MMU-RAGent competition at NeurIPS 2025, where it was awarded Best Dynamic Evaluation in the text-to-text track. Rather than targeting...

item.title: Detecting AI-Generated Content on Social Media with Multi-modal Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2606.11200v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Generative AI has enabled the creation of photorealistic images and videos that are increasingly disseminated on social media, often used for spam, misinformation, manipulation, and fraud. Existing AI-generated content (AIGC) detection methods face...

item.title: One Jailbreak, Many Tongues: Learning Language-Insensitive Intention Representations for Multilingual Jailbreak Detection
item.description: arXiv:2606.11202v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed in applications for global multilingual users, yet safety training remains concentrated in dominant languages and has not progressed in parallel with multilingual capability, creating exploitable...

item.title: [AINews] Open Models, Model Labs vs Agent Labs, and What's Untrainable — Sarah Guo
item.description: a quiet day lets us reflect on a great essay

item.title: ByteDance Spins Off AI Drug Discovery Business for Independent Financing, AI4S Enters Industrial Phase
item.description: ByteDance is spinning off its AI drug discovery business for independent financing, retaining majority control as the company commercializes its AI capabilities in life sciences.

item.title: MIIT Calls for Strengthened R&D of High-End Optoelectronic Chips in New AI Communications Policy Plan
item.description: China's MIIT issued a 2026-2028 policy plan calling for accelerated R&D of high-end optoelectronic chips, 800Gbps backbone networks, and AI-compatible communications infrastructure.

item.title: StoreClaw Aims to Unify AI-Powered Cross-Border E-Commerce Operations Across Amazon, Shopify, and TikTok Shop
item.description: StoreClaw, a new AI startup that achieved Product Hunt #1 rankings, aims to replace fragmented cross-border e-commerce tools with a unified multi-channel AI platform.

item.title: Alibaba Returns to Centralized Power Structure as AI Reshapes Organizational Logic
item.description: Alibaba is undergoing its most significant organizational shift in years, consolidating AI capabilities under CEO Wu Yongming's direct command as the company pivots from a decentralized business model to an AI-driven token platform.

item.title: Insta360 Launches Luna Ultra 8K Gimbal Camera and Welcomes Smartphone Makers to the Category
item.description: Insta360 has officially entered the handheld gimbal camera market with the Luna Ultra 8K flagship, positioning the device as an AI-powered 'cameraman' rather than a DJI Pocket competitor, while welcoming smartphone makers into the category.

item.title: Image Resizing on R2 issues
item.description: Jun 11, 01:43 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jun 11, 01:31 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jun 10, 23:46 UTC Identified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented. Jun 10, 23:44 UTC Investigating - Cloudflare has...

item.title: INC20000032
item.description: Jun 11, 00:30 UTC Resolved - Current status: We identified and reverted a misconfigured change, and monitored the environment to confirm that service was restored. If you experience additional issues or have questions, please open a support case via Snowflake Community or the Support page in...

item.title: Pages Build & Deployment Delays
item.description: Jun 10, 23:46 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jun 10, 22:18 UTC Investigating - Cloudflare Pages customers may be experiencing delays in builds, project creation, or deployments.

item.title: Network Connectivity issues in Western North America impacting R2 and Containers
item.description: Jun 10, 18:26 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jun 10, 17:01 UTC Update - We are continuing to investigate this issue. Jun 10, 16:35 UTC Investigating - Customers may see increased latency while using R2 buckets and scheduling containers in the Western...

item.title: Cloudflare Images upload failures
item.description: Jun 10, 17:21 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jun 10, 15:53 UTC Investigating - Cloudflare is aware of, and investigating an issue which potentially impacts customers trying to upload new files to Cloudflare Images.We are working to understand the full...

item.title: DiffusionGemma: 4x faster text generation
China-Linked JDY Botnet Expands to 1,500+ Devices for Cyber Reconnaissance
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have warned of a "resurgence and expansion" of JDY, a covert network associated with China-nexus state-sponsored threat actors. "The JDY botnet comprises over 1,500 SOHO [small office and home office] and IoT devices and operates as a centrally controlled, high-performance...

item.title: Authentication issues related to API requests
item.description: Jun 10, 15:27 UTC Update - We are investigating issues related to sporadic authentication failures impacting approximately 15% of API traffic. We will continue to investigate and provide updates. Jun 10, 15:27 UTC Update - Issues is experiencing degraded performance. We are continuing to...

item.title: Ivanti, Fortinet, and SAP Release Patches for Multiple Critical Vulnerabilities
item.description: Fortinet, Ivanti, and SAP have released security updates to address multiple critical security vulnerabilities that could result in arbitrary code execution and information disclosure. The security flaw patched by Fortinet relates to a command injection vulnerability in FortiSandbox, FortiSandbox...

item.title: Unpatched Langflow Flaw CVE-2026-5027 Exploited for Unauthenticated RCE
item.description: A high-severity unpatched security flaw in Langflow, an open-source low-code platform to build artificial intelligence (AI) applications, has come under active exploitation in the wild, according to findings from VulnCheck. The vulnerability in question is CVE-2026-5027 (CVSS score: 8.8), a case of...

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude Haiku 4.5
item.description: Jun 10, 14:59 UTC Update - We are continuing to work on a fix for this issue. Jun 10, 13:19 UTC Identified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented. Jun 10, 13:06 UTC Investigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: CISA Adds Cisco, Chrome, and Arista Flaws to KEV Catalog Amid Active Exploitation
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Tuesday added three new vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, following reports of active exploitation. The list of vulnerabilities is as follows - CVE-2026-20245 (CVSS score: 7.8) - An improper...

item.title: Who Runs the Ransomware Group ‘The Gentlemen?’
item.description: A cybercrime group known as The Gentlemen has emerged as the second most active ransomware gang by victim count, rapidly attracting a talented pool of hackers through an aggressive recruitment strategy that promises affiliates 90 percent of any ransom paid by victims. This post examines clues...

item.title: Infostealers Turn Millions of Devices Into Credential Theft Machines
item.description: As attackers increasingly favor stolen credentials over exploits, infostealers have become a primary source of access for ransomware and other cybercrime operations. The post Infostealers Turn Millions of Devices Into Credential Theft Machines appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Cyera Raises $600 Million at $12 Billion Valuation
item.description: Cyera is positioned as one of the most valuable privately held cybersecurity firms in the world with total funding topping $2 billion. The post Cyera Raises $600 Million at $12 Billion Valuation appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Aryon Security Raises $29 Million in Series A Funding
item.description: In the post-Mythos era, the company’s platform helps organizations enforce security controls across environments. The post Aryon Security Raises $29 Million in Series A Funding appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: The Download: the “steroid olympics” and a safer Mythos
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. The “steroid olympics” were a circus—and a window into our culture —Amit Katwala A couple of weeks ago, at a $50 million arena built in a casino...

item.title: Critical HVAC and UPS Vulnerabilities Could Let Hackers Disrupt Data Centers
item.description: Claroty researchers have analyzed the security of Vertiv UPS network cards and the Trane Tracer SC+ HVAC controller. The post Critical HVAC and UPS Vulnerabilities Could Let Hackers Disrupt Data Centers appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: CISO Forum Webinar Today: 2026 Mid-Year Review
item.description: Learn more about protecting against unmonitored use of generative AI (Shadow AI) in business units and building and enforcing AI governance frameworks. The post CISO Forum Webinar Today: 2026 Mid-Year Review appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Increased HTTP 526 Errors
item.description: Jun 10, 11:41 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jun 10, 10:50 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jun 10, 10:20 UTC Identified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented. Jun 10, 09:36 UTC Investigating - Cloudflare is...

item.title: Your Automated Pentest Looks Clean. See What It Missed in This Expert Webinar
item.description: Your pentest report looks clean. That might be the problem. Run automated pentesting long enough, and the new findings start to dry up. By the third or fourth run, fewer issues appear. The report looks stable. Leadership reads "stable" as "secure." It usually isn't. The work slows down. The risk...

item.title: The “steroid olympics” were a circus—and a window into our culture
item.description: Testosterone. Methenolone. Nandrolone. Human growth hormone and EPO. Meldonium, modafinil, and mixed amphetamine salts. Clomiphene, anastrozole, levothyroxine, and liothyronine. Patches and capsules, creams and pills. A whole galaxy of steroids, metabolic modulators, and synthetic hormones coursing...

item.title: [AINews] Anthropic Claude Fable 5 — Mythos but Safe, with Controversial Terms
item.description: The much anticipated launch of the Mythos-class model was marred by some controversial usage policies

item.title: UPDATE: Network traffic to Google Cloud originating from Delhi, Chennai, Mumbai and surrounding areas is experiencing intermittent periods of elevated latency and possible packet loss.
item.description: Incident began at 2026-06-09 11:22 (all times are US/Pacific).Summary Network traffic to Google Cloud originating from Delhi, Chennai, Mumbai and surrounding areas is experiencing intermittent periods of elevated latency and possible packet loss. Description A fire at a third-party data center...

item.title: Claude Fable 5 and new AI safety fables
item.description: One step further into the power politics of frontier AI systems.

item.title: A Record-Breaking Patch Tuesday for June 2026
item.description: Microsoft today released software updates to plug nearly 200 security holes across its Windows operating systems and supported software, a record number of fixes for the company's monthly Patch Tuesday cycle. Nearly three dozen of those bugs earned Microsoft's most dire "critical" rating, and...

item.title: Fable not available in the model picker in Claude Code Desktop
item.description: Jun 9, 21:03 UTC Resolved - This issue has been resolved. If you are experiencing issues with selecting Claude Fable within the Claude Code Desktop application, please ensure you have updated to the latest version. Jun 9, 20:16 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the...

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude Opus 4.6
item.description: Jun 9, 20:32 UTC Investigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: Can Voice Agents Handle Bilingual Customers? Benchmarking Frontier ASR on Code-Switched Speech
